lessons.title: What is Project Management?
lessons.description: Distinguish projects (temporary, unique) from operations (ongoing, repetitive)

lessons.title: The Project Manager's Role
lessons.description: Understand the PM's three core responsibilities: Plan, Execute, Monitor & Control

lessons.title: Project Stakeholders
lessons.description: Define who stakeholders are and why early identification is critical

lessons.title: Project Life Cycles
lessons.description: Understand the three main project life cycles: Predictive, Adaptive (Agile), and Hybrid

lessons.title: Organizational Structures
lessons.description: Understand the three main organizational structures: Functional, Matrix, and Projectized

lessons.title: Project Constraints
lessons.description: Identify the six major project constraints: Scope, Schedule, Cost, Quality, Resources, and Risk

lessons.title: Project Success & Value
lessons.description: Distinguish between traditional project success (on time, on budget, meets scope) and modern value-driven success (business outcomes, stakeholder satisfaction, benefits realization)

lessons.title: Project Integration Basics
lessons.description: Understand Project Integration Management as the HOLISTIC view connecting all 10 knowledge areas

lessons.title: Stakeholder Identification & Prioritization
lessons.description: Identify key stakeholders in a project context using systematic stakeholder identification techniques

lessons.title: Stakeholder Power & Interest Grid
lessons.description: Classify stakeholders using the Power/Interest Grid into 4 quadrants: Manage Closely, Keep Satisfied, Keep Informed, Monitor

lessons.title: Stakeholder Engagement Planning
lessons.description: Apply the Stakeholder Engagement Continuum: Unaware, Resistant, Neutral, Supportive, Leading

lessons.title: Communication Methods & Channels
lessons.description: Calculate communication channels using the formula n(n-1)/2 and understand exponential growth

lessons.title: Managing Difficult Stakeholders
lessons.description: Apply negotiation strategies (Win-Win, Win-Lose, Withdraw) based on relationship importance and power dynamics

lessons.title: Effective Communication Planning
lessons.description: Select appropriate communication methods (Push, Pull, Interactive) based on stakeholder needs

lessons.title: Team Formation - Tuckman's Model
lessons.description: Identify the 5 stages of Tuckman's team development model: Forming, Storming, Norming, Performing, and Adjourning

lessons.title: Building High-Performance Teams
lessons.description: Recognize the 5 characteristics of high-performance teams: Trust, Clear Goals, Psychological Safety, Shared Accountability, Continuous Improvement

lessons.title: Conflict Resolution Strategies
lessons.description: Identify the five conflict resolution strategies (Collaborate/Confront, Compromise, Smooth/Accommodate, Force/Direct, Withdraw/Avoid) and their outcomes (win-win, lose-lose, win-lose, yield-lose, lose-leave)

lessons.title: Leadership Styles
lessons.description: Identify the five core leadership styles: Servant, Transformational, Transactional, Laissez-faire, and Autocratic/Directive

lessons.title: Motivation & Recognition
lessons.description: Diagnose whether a team member's motivation issue is intrinsic (challenge, growth, autonomy) or extrinsic (recognition, rewards, hygiene)

lessons.title: Project Charter Development
lessons.description: Understand the purpose and authority of the project charter as the formal document that authorizes the project

lessons.title: Project Management Plan
lessons.description: Understand that the Project Management Plan integrates ALL subsidiary plans into one cohesive document

lessons.title: Direct & Manage Project Work
lessons.description: Understand that Direct & Manage Project Work is THE primary execution process where plans become deliverables

lessons.title: Managing Project Knowledge
lessons.description: Distinguish between explicit knowledge (documented, codified) and tacit knowledge (experience-based, hard to articulate)

lessons.title: Monitor & Control Project Work
lessons.description: Distinguish between monitoring (measuring performance against baselines) and controlling (taking corrective/preventive action)

lessons.title: Integrated Change Control
lessons.description: Understand Integrated Change Control as the formal APPROVAL process for all change requests affecting baselines

lessons.title: Project Closure
lessons.description: Understand project closure as a FORMAL process, not just work completion

lessons.title: Benefits Realization
lessons.description: Distinguish project success (time, budget, scope) from business success (benefits realized)

lessons.title: Cost Estimation & Budgeting
lessons.description: Select the appropriate estimation technique (bottom-up, analogous, parametric, three-point) based on data availability and detail requirements

lessons.title: Earned Value Management
lessons.description: Calculate core EVM metrics: PV (Planned Value), EV (Earned Value), AC (Actual Cost)

lessons.title: Critical Path Method
lessons.description: Identify the critical path as the longest path through a network diagram with zero float

lessons.title: Schedule Compression Techniques
lessons.description: Distinguish between fast tracking (parallelizing sequential activities) and crashing (adding resources to reduce duration)

lessons.title: Risk Identification & Analysis
lessons.description: Select the appropriate risk identification technique based on team dynamics (Brainstorming, Delphi, SWOT, Assumptions Analysis)

lessons.title: Risk Response Planning
lessons.description: Match risk characteristics to the appropriate response strategy (Avoid, Mitigate, Transfer, Accept, Escalate)

lessons.title: Risk Monitoring & Control
lessons.description: Distinguish between residual risks (remain after response), secondary risks (caused by response), and new risks (emerge during execution)

lessons.title: Quality Planning & Assurance
lessons.description: Distinguish between Quality Assurance (defect prevention) and Quality Control (defect detection)

lessons.title: Quality Control & Continuous Improvement
lessons.description: Distinguish between inspection (defect detection) and prevention (defect avoidance) and understand prevention's cost effectiveness

lessons.title: Procurement & Contracts
lessons.description: Select the appropriate contract type (Fixed Price, Cost Reimbursable, Time & Materials) based on scope clarity and risk tolerance

lessons.title: Scrum Fundamentals & Roles
lessons.description: Understand the 3 Scrum Roles: Product Owner, Scrum Master, Development Team

lessons.title: Agile Team Formation & Dynamics
lessons.description: Apply the optimal Agile team size principle (5-9 members, the 7±2 rule)

lessons.title: Sprint Planning & Execution
lessons.description: Understand two-part Sprint Planning: WHAT (selecting items) + HOW (task breakdown)

lessons.title: Daily Standup & Collaboration
lessons.description: Recognize Daily Standup as TEAM synchronization, not a PM/PO status meeting

lessons.title: Sprint Review & Demos
lessons.description: Distinguish Sprint Review (collaboration for feedback) from formal approval gates

lessons.title: Sprint Retrospectives
lessons.description: Distinguish Sprint Retrospective (process improvement) from Sprint Review (product feedback)

lessons.title: Agile Principles & Values
lessons.description: Apply the 4 Agile Manifesto values to decide when Agile is appropriate vs predictive approaches

lessons.title: Predictive vs Adaptive
lessons.description: Select the appropriate development approach (predictive, adaptive, hybrid) based on project characteristics

lessons.title: User Story Writing - INVEST Criteria
lessons.description: Apply INVEST criteria (Independent, Negotiable, Valuable, Estimable, Small, Testable) to evaluate user story quality

lessons.title: Backlog Refinement & Prioritization
lessons.description: Apply MoSCoW prioritization (Must/Should/Could/Won't-have) to balance business value with scope constraints

lessons.title: Kanban Fundamentals — Visualize Flow, Limit Work
lessons.description: Identify the 5 core Kanban principles: visualize workflow, limit WIP, manage flow, make policies explicit, implement feedback loops

lessons.title: Agile Estimation Techniques
lessons.description: Apply Planning Poker to estimate user stories using relative sizing

lessons.title: Agile Metrics & Tracking
lessons.description: Calculate and interpret team velocity to predict delivery timelines

lessons.title: Definition of Done — The Quality Gate
lessons.description: Create a team Definition of Done with quality standards that apply to every user story
